behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS applies: a unit test of JSON parsing alone can miss failures in the handoff from parser output into hook input handling. No real OS/network/subprocess is required, so System is unnecessary.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real hook-input handling entry function with in-process payload fixtures that include code fences, backticks, and `$vars` inside JSON strings. Assert parse success, no `json_parse_failed` trace, and normal downstream handling path is taken. Avoid mocking parser/handler boundary.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: This checks parser error details plus trace-record construction together. A pure unit mock of parse errors could hide real error-shape/offset mismatches, so Integration is the minimum realistic boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Feed malformed JSON fixtures (e.g., missing quote/comma, truncated object) into the real parse+trace path in-process. Assert a `json_parse_failed` trace is emitted and includes diagnostic context (e.g., error message and location/snippet fields) needed to identify malformed region.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans parse failure propagation into policy mapping and telemetry intent tagging across module boundaries. Unit tests on policy alone could miss wiring failures.
behaviors.test_description: Trigger a real parse failure through the local hook-input path and assert returned handled result (no exception escapes), plus telemetry intent flag/state is set as expected. Use real policy and telemetry-intent components in-process.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA applies directly: subprocess stdin/exit behavior is the failure boundary. In-process mocks can hide crashes, buffering issues, or signal-loss on process teardown.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the hook as a subprocess, pipe malformed stdin, wait for exit, and assert process stays controlled (no unhandled crash semantics) and emits expected parse-failure trace signal before exit.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: End-to-end hook invocation with real stdin and real trace sink crosses process/I/O boundaries; MOCK-HIDE applies because mocked sink/process can pass while real artifact emission fails.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real hook binary/script with malformed stdin and configured real trace sink target (e.g., file/pipe used in production path). Assert resulting trace artifact exists and contains `json_parse_failed` marker.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: Requires side-by-side execution of both classes through the same real handling pipeline to catch attribution bugs in classification/trace logic. No external infra or model dependence is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Run a parameterized regression suite through the real in-process parse+classification/trace path: (a) valid JSON with markdown-heavy strings, (b) malformed JSON. Assert only malformed cases produce `json_parse_failed`; valid-markdown cases follow success path.
